ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2017 Continued To Aug-29-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar May-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Mynor Hernandez' motion to quash service of summon is granted. Plaintiff Vilma Lozano has not presented any admissible evidence that Mr. Hernandez was properly served by substitute service at his residence, place of business or mailing address. While this motion is meritorious as a matter of law, it serves no useful purpose other than to delay the case and add to everyone's costs. Eventually Mr. Hernandez will be served and he will be in no better position than he would be in today than if he did not file this motion. The court strongly encourages Mr. Hernandez' counsel to accept service on Mr. Hernandez' behalf to avoid his having to pay for costs of service. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
